english: Good morning! A hot macchiato coffee.
choicesEnglish: Good morning! A hot macchiato coffee.
correctAnswerEnglish: Good morning! A hot macchiato coffee.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una delle ordinazioni più comuni al mattino.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo un espresso con un goccio di latte caldo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! One of the most common orders in the morning.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking for an espresso with a drop of hot milk.

italian: Lo vuole con latte freddo a parte?
english: Do you want it with cold milk on the side?
choicesItalian: Lo vuole con latte freddo a parte?
Lo perde con borsa nera a via?
Lo ride con news finta a re?
Lo corre con gita lunga a porto?
choicesEnglish: Do you want it with cold milk on the side?
Do you lose it with a black bag on the street?
Do you laugh at it with fake news to the king?
Do you run it with a long trip to the port?
correctAnswerItalian: Lo vuole con latte freddo a parte?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you want it with cold milk on the side?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una domanda di cortesia del barista per personalizzare il caffè.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti chiede se desideri aggiungere latte non riscaldato separatamente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A polite question from the barista to customize the coffee.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it asks if you want to add unheated milk separately.

italian: Macchiato direttamente, con un po' di schiuma.
english: Macchiato directly, with a bit of foam.
choicesItalian: Macchiato direttamente, con un po' di schiuma.
Sporcato via, con un po' di fango.
Gridato news, con un po' di baci.
Corso gita, con un po' di tour.
choicesEnglish: Macchiato directly, with a bit of foam.
Dirtied street, with a bit of mud.
Shouted news, with a bit of kisses.
Run trip, with a bit of tour.
correctAnswerItalian: Macchiato direttamente, con un po' di schiuma.
correctAnswerEnglish: Macchiato directly, with a bit of foam.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Specificare che il latte deve essere già nel caffè con la sua schiuma.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, preferisci il caffè già preparato dal barista.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Specifying that the milk should already be in the coffee with its foam.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you prefer the coffee already prepared by the barista.

english: Do you have jam pastries?
choicesEnglish: Do you have bags on the street?
Do you have guides at the tower?
Do you have news at the square?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedere la disponibilità del dolce ripieno di confettura.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai cercando i cornetti con la frutta dolce.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking for the availability of the jam-filled sweet.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for croissants with sweet fruit.

english: Must I pay first at the register?
choicesEnglish: Must I pay first at the register?
Must I lose later on the street?
Must I always laugh at the news?
Must I run now to the trip?
correctAnswerEnglish: Must I pay first at the register?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una domanda fondamentale per capire le regole del bar italiano.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se devi fare lo scontrino prima di consumare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A fundamental question to understand the rules of the Italian bar.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know if you have to get the receipt before consuming.

italian: In Italia si paga spesso prima di ordinare al banco.
english: In Italy one often pays before ordering at the counter.
choicesItalian: In Italia si paga spesso prima di ordinare al banco.
In via si perde raro prima di ridere al bar.
In cassa si vende sempre dopo di correre al tour.
In news si rida finto prima di gridare al re.
choicesEnglish: In Italy one often pays before ordering at the counter.
On the street one rarely loses before laughing at the bar.
At the register one always sells after running to the tour.
In the news one laughs fake before shouting at the king.
correctAnswerItalian: In Italia si paga spesso prima di ordinare al banco.
correctAnswerEnglish: In Italy one often pays before ordering at the counter.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un'osservazione corretta sulle abitudini locali.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, descrive l'usanza tipica di fare lo scontrino preventivo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A correct observation on local habits.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it describes the typical custom of getting a preliminary receipt.

italian: Faccia lo scontrino là in fondo.
english: Get the receipt over there at the back.
choicesItalian: Faccia lo scontrino là in fondo.
Perda la borsa là in via.
Gridi la news là in piazza.
Corra la gita là in porto.
choicesEnglish: Get the receipt over there at the back.
Lose the bag over there on the street.
Shout the news over there in the square.
Run the trip over there at the port.
correctAnswerItalian: Faccia lo scontrino là in fondo.
correctAnswerEnglish: Get the receipt over there at the back.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'indicazione del barista verso la cassa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti sta dicendo dove andare per pagare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A direction from the barista toward the cash register.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he is telling you where to go to pay.

english: Come back here with the receipt.
choicesEnglish: Come back here with the receipt.
correctAnswerEnglish: Come back here with the receipt.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! L'invito a presentare la prova di pagamento al banco.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il barista aspetta che tu gli mostri il foglietto fiscale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The invitation to present proof of payment at the counter.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the barista is waiting for you to show him the fiscal slip.

english: What will you have?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! La domanda standard del cassiere o del barista.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti sta chiedendo cosa desideri ordinare o pagare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The standard question from the cashier or barista.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he is asking you what you wish to order or pay for.

english: Here is the receipt.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Consegnare il pezzo di carta al barista per avere l'ordine.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai mostrando di aver pagato alla cassa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Handing the piece of paper to the barista to get the order.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're showing you paid at the register.

english: The macchiato is being prepared.
choicesEnglish: The mud is in the bag.
The news is in the square.
The trip is in the port.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un avviso che il caffè sta arrivando.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che il barista sta lavorando sulla tua ordinazione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A notice that the coffee is coming.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that the barista is working on your order.

english: Can you give me a glass of water?
choicesItalian: Mi perde una borsa di fango?
Mi rida una news di baci?
Mi corre una gita di tour?
choicesEnglish: Can you give me a glass of water?
Can you lose a bag of mud?
Can you give me back some news of kisses?
Can you run a trip of tours?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can you give me a glass of water?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! In molti bar italiani l'acqua col caffè è gratis al banco.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo qualcosa da bere per sciacquare la bocca.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! In many Italian bars, water with coffee is free at the counter.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking for something to drink to rinse your mouth.

english: Enjoy your breakfast!

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un augurio cordiale per il primo pasto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai augurando piacere nel mangiare al mattino.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A cordial wish for the first meal.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are wishing pleasure in eating in the morning.

english: I don't drink caffeine, I prefer barley (coffee).
choicesItalian: Non perdo borsa, scelgo la via.
Non rido news, temo il re.
Non corro gita, odio il tour.
choicesEnglish: I don't drink caffeine, I prefer barley coffee.
I don't lose the bag, I choose the street.
I don't laugh at news, I fear the king.
I don't run the trip, I hate the tour.
correctAnswerEnglish: I don't drink caffeine, I prefer barley coffee.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Spiegare una restrizione o preferenza alimentare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai ordinando un'alternativa calda al caffè espresso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Explaining a food restriction or preference.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are ordering a hot alternative to espresso coffee.

italian: Lo vuole in tazza grande o piccola?
english: Do you want it in a large or small cup?
choicesItalian: Lo vuole in tazza grande o piccola?
Lo perde in borsa nera o rossa?
Lo rida in news finta o vera?
Lo corre in gita lunga o breve?
choicesEnglish: Do you want it in a large or small cup?
Do you lose it in a black or red bag?
Do you give it back in fake or true news?
Do you run it in a long or short trip?
correctAnswerItalian: Lo vuole in tazza grande o piccola?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you want it in a large or small cup?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere la dimensione del contenitore per la bevanda.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il barista vuole sapere la pezzatura della tazza.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking for the container's size for the drink.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the barista wants to know the cup's size.

english: At the counter or seated?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Una distinzione importante per il prezzo in Italia.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiede se vuoi stare in piedi o al tavolo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! An important distinction for price in Italy.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it asks if you want to stand or be at the table.

english: I'd like a slice of cake.
choicesEnglish: I'd like a slice of cake.
I'd like a piece of mud.
I'd like a bag of gifts.
I'd like a piece of news about kisses.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'd like a slice of cake.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una richiesta golosa per colazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai ordinando una porzione di dolce da forno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A tasty breakfast request.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are ordering a portion of baked sweet.

english: No sugar, thanks.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Preferire la bevanda al naturale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai rifiutando il dolcificante.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Preferring the drink plain.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are refusing the sweetener.

english: As for the barley coffee, I drink it bitter.
choicesItalian: Per la borsa, la perdo sporca.
Per la news, la rido finta.
Per la gita, la corro lunga.
choicesEnglish: As for the barley coffee, I drink it bitter.
As for the bag, I lose it dirty.
As for the news, I laugh at it fake.
As for the trip, I run it long.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Specificare che non vuoi aggiungere zucchero.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, preferisci il gusto deciso del cereale tostato.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Specifying that you don't want to add sugar.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you prefer the strong taste of the roasted grain.

english: The biscuits look good.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un complimento all'aspetto dei dolci esposti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai notando che i frollini hanno un bell'aspetto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A compliment on the appearance of the displayed sweets.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're noticing that the shortbread looks nice.

english: Do you want to taste one?
choicesEnglish: Do you want to taste one?
Where do you want to run one?
When do you want to laugh at one?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you want to taste one?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! L'offerta del barista di una piccola prova gratuita.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti sta proponendo un campione omaggio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The barista's offer of a small free trial.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's proposing a free sample.

english: It's a free gift.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Quando qualcosa non costa nulla.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa offerto gratuitamente dalla casa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! When something costs nothing.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means offered for free by the establishment.

english: Enjoy your stay.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un augurio cortese per chi resta nel locale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai augurando un buon proseguimento del tempo trascorso qui.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A polite wish for those staying in the venue.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are wishing a good continuation of the time spent here.

english: I'd like a fresh orange juice.
choicesEnglish: I'd like a fresh orange juice.
I'd like an old dirty beer.
I'd like a new long street.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere una bevanda fatta sul momento con gli agrumi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai ordinando del succo d'arancia appena spremuto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking for a drink made on the spot with citrus fruits.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are ordering freshly squeezed orange juice.

english: I'm thirsty.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Esprimere il bisogno di bere acqua o altro.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica la necessità di liquidi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Expressing the need to drink water or something else.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the need for liquids.

italian: Posso avere il latte freddo a parte?
english: Can I have the cold milk on the side?
choicesItalian: Posso avere il latte freddo a parte?
Posso perdere la borsa sporca là?

choicesEnglish: Can I have the cold milk on the side?

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking not to mix the milk with the coffee immediately.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want the milk in a separate container.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Quando la temperatura scotta e non si può bere.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica un calore eccessivo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! When the temperature burns and you can't drink.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates excessive heat.

english: A white coffee (latte) would be better.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Scegliere una bevanda con più latte rispetto al macchiato.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, preferisci la tazza grande con molto latte e poco caffè.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Choosing a drink with more milk than a macchiato.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you prefer the large cup with a lot of milk and little coffee.

english: What can I make for you (plural)?
choicesEnglish: What can I make for you?
Where is he running to you?
When is he laughing at you?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! La domanda del barista rivolta a un gruppo di persone.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiede cosa desiderano bere o mangiare gli amici.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The barista's question addressed to a group of people.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it asks what the friends wish to drink or eat.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica la combinazione tipica di fette di pane condite.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the typical combination of seasoned bread slices.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, propone di scaldare le fette di pane.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he proposes to heat the bread slices.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Specificare la preferenza per il pane scuro.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiedi la versione con farina non raffinata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Specifying the preference for dark bread.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking for the version with unrefined flour.

english: I'll give you the bill.
choicesEnglish: I'll lose the kiss for you.
I'll call the king for you.

correctAnswerEnglish: I'll calculate the bill for you.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Il barista calcola quanto dovete pagare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, sta sommando i prezzi dei prodotti scelti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The barista calculates how much you have to pay.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he's summing the prices of the chosen products.

english: Unfortunately they are finished / ran out.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Avvisare che il prodotto richiesto non è più disponibile.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che le scorte sono terminate.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Warning that the requested product is no longer available.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that stocks have run out.

choicesItalian: I biscotti fatti in casa sono squisiti.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, lodi il sapore eccezionale dei frollini prodotti nel bar.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A sincere compliment to the artisanal quality.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're praising the exceptional taste of the shortbread produced in the bar.

english: I make them myself every morning.
choicesEnglish: I make them myself every morning.
I laugh at them every news.
correctAnswerEnglish: I make them myself every morning.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, specifica che è lui stesso a produrre i dolci quotidianamente.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he specifies that he himself produces the sweets daily.

choicesEnglish: Where do you want a gift?

feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking if you want two pieces of something.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he proposes buying a pair of biscuits.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai lodando il sapore dei dolci appena mangiati.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Expressing appreciation for the pastry chef's work.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're praising the flavor of the sweets just eaten.

english: To take away.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Quando vuoi che il cibo sia confezionato per uscire.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che non consumerai i prodotti nel locale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! When you want the food to be packaged to go out.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates you won't consume the products on the premises.

choicesItalian: Me li metta in un sacchetto.

Laugh them off in a news.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi che i biscotti siano inseriti nel sacchettino.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking for the paper container for transport.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want the biscuits to be put in the small bag.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che hai pochissimo tempo a disposizione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Communicating the order's urgency.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate that you have very little time available.

english: Can you give me a napkin?
choicesEnglish: Can you give me a napkin?
Can you give me back a kiss?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerchi lo strumento per rimuovere tracce di cibo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking for paper to clean yourself.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for the tool to remove food traces.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Ammettere di aver macchiato i vestiti col cibo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che hai tracce di polvere o cibo addosso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Admitting you stained your clothes with food.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates you have traces of dust or food on you.

english: Have a good day.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è la formula di cortesia finale del mattino.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The classic greeting when leaving the bar.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica un'altissima temperatura del liquido.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! When the drink is too hot to be drunk.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates a very high temperature of the liquid.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi raffreddare il caffè con pochissimo latte.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking for a minimal amount of unheated milk.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to cool the coffee with a little milk.

feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means the order is about to be served.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è l'atto di porgere il liquido trasparente.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il barista ti elenca le opzioni di ripieno disponibili.
